2606.10504.title: Cross-Modal Knowledge Distillation without Paired Data: Theoretical Foundation and Algorithm
2606.10504.paper_summary: Cross-modal knowledge distillation (CMKD) studies how a (large) teacher model trained on one type of data (e.g., images) can guide a (smaller) student model building on another type of data (e.g., text/audio). Existing CMKD methods often require paired multi-modal data with aligned semantics, but obtaining such paired data are often costly and impractical. To mitigate this limitation, we develop a new CMKD framework for the more challenging setting where paired data are unavailable. In particular, we establish a cross-modal distributional relationship between teacher and student models, which reveals two fundamental quantities governing effective distillation: feature alignment and label alignment. These quantities characterize semantic discrepancy between modalities at the levels of representation and prediction distributions, respectively. Motivated by this insight, we propose a principled framework, with theoretical guarantees, that enables effective cross-modal knowledge distillation by aligning distributions rather than individual samples. Extensive experiments across a wide range of multimodal benchmarks show that our framework is highly effective in both unpaired and paired data settings, improving significantly over prior work.
2606.10504.paper_summary_zh: 跨模態知識蒸餾 (CMKD) 研究如何在一種數據類型（例如圖像）上訓練的（大型）教師模型可以指導在另一種數據類型（例如文本/音頻）上構建的（較小型）學生模型。現有的 CMKD 方法通常需要具有對齊語義的配對多模態數據，但獲得這樣的配對數據往往成本高昂且不切實際。為了減輕這一限制，我們開發了一個新的 CMKD 框架，適用於配對數據不可用的更具挑戰性的情境。具體而言，我們建立了教師和學生模型之間的跨模態分佈關係，這揭示了兩個支配有效蒸餾的基本量：特徵對齊和標籤對齊。這些量分別表徵了表示和預測分佈層面上模態之間的語義差異。受到這一見解的啟發，我們提出了一個有理論保證的原則框架，通過對齊分佈而非單個樣本來實現有效的跨模態知識蒸餾。在廣泛的多模態基準測試中進行的廣泛實驗表明，我們的框架在配對和非配對數據設置中都非常有效，顯著超越了先前的工作。
2606.10504.author: Trong Khiem Tran et.al.
2606.10504.authors: Trong Khiem Tran, Anh Duc Chu, Quang Hung Pham, Phi Le Nguyen, Trong Nghia Hoang
